prompt: The subclass constructor does not compile. What is the bug?
choices: Sub must declare a matching constructor with the same parameter type
When Base defines only a parameterized constructor, Java does not generate a default no-arg constructor; Sub() must explicitly call super(n)
Subclass constructors are not allowed when the parent has a constructor
Sub must call this() before super()
explanation: Java generates a default no-arg constructor only if no constructor is defined. Base has Base(int n) so no default is generated. Sub() implicitly calls super() (no-arg), which does not exist — compile error. Fix: Sub() { super(0); ... } supplying a required argument.
